contentBlocks.original:
हिमकुन्द मृणालाभं दैत्यानां परमं गुरुम् ।
सर्वशास्त्र प्रवक्तारं भार्गवं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
himakunda mṛṇāḻābhaṃ daityānāṃ paramaṃ gurum ।
sarvaśāstra pravaktāraṃ bhārgavaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Bhārgava (Venus), bright as snow, the lotus stalk, and kuśa grass, the supreme preceptor of the demons, the teacher of all scriptures. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
शुक्लाम्बरं शुक्ल माल्यं शुक्ल गन्धानुलेपनम् ।
वज्र माणिक्य भूषाढ्यं किरीट मकुटोज्ज्वलम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śuklāmbaraṃ śukla mālyaṃ śukla gandhānulēpanam ।
vajra māṇikya bhūṣāḍhyaṃ kirīṭa makuṭōjjvalam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Wearing white robes and white garlands, anointed with white sandal paste, adorned with diamond and ruby ornaments, resplendent with a jewelled crown and diadem — (2)

contentBlocks.original:
श्वेताम्बर श्वेतवपुश्चतुर्भुज समन्वितः ।
रत्न सिंहासनारूडो रथस्थोरजतप्रभः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śvētāmbara śvētavapuśchaturbhuja samanvitaḥ ।
ratna siṃhāsanārūḍō rathasthōrajataprabhaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: White-robed, of white body, endowed with four arms, seated on a jewelled lion-throne, riding a chariot, of silver radiance — (3)

contentBlocks.original:
भृगुर्भोगकरो भूमीसुरपालन तत्परः ।
सर्वैश्वर्य प्रद स्वर्वगीर्वाण गणसन्नुतः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhṛgurbhōgakarō bhūmīsurapālana tatparaḥ ।
sarvaiśvarya prada svarvagīrvāṇa gaṇasannutaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bhṛgu, the giver of all enjoyment, ever devoted to protecting the gods upon the earth; bestower of all prosperity, praised by the hosts of heaven — (4)

contentBlocks.original:
दण्डहस्तञ्च वरदां भानुज्वालाङ्ग शोभितम् ।
अक्षमाला कमण्डलं देवं तं भार्गवं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daṇḍahastañcha varadāṃ bhānujvālāṅga śōbhitam ।
akṣamālā kamaṇḍalaṃ dēvaṃ taṃ bhārgavaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to that Bhārgava, holding a staff, with boon-bestowing gestures, adorned with divine radiance, bearing a rosary and a water pot. (5)
